entry.title: Archiving - Optimize for PDI, Part 2
entry.content:
The Archiving process needs to retrieve all data, including PDI and Extension Fields, before sending them to the archive.
Therefore all PDI scripts are performed during this retrieve operation.

But when the archiving starts, i.e. the ArchivingStatusCode is set to InProgress and later to Archived, the update shall only on this code value. This is done so any process agent which is listen to this, can start.

With the current release 2408 we suppress the execution of any PDI script during this status update.
This mechanism is the same as already applied on suppressing the execution of validations, both SAP and PDI.
According to our knowledge (and your complaints 😉 ) this will speed up the process enormously.

Of course the OnDelete script will always be executed when the instance is deleted, so you can react and cleanup any shadowing Custom Business Objects or data sources or ...

entry.title: Generating parquet file to use APM File Upload API
entry.content:
Introduction

This document outlines the standards and procedures for creating a data file (parquet fileformat) to ingest data for equipments in APM. Adherence to these guidelines ensures file-data compatibility and optimal performance.

Timeseries File Upload API creates IoT time series data for a combination of technicalObjectNumber, technicalObjectType, SSID, positionID, and categoryName within a particular time range.

 

File Structure

managedObjectId
measuringNodeId
_time
C_1101
C_1102

04ef86bd-ae54-4930-8006-ae6d91f80692

789f706a-f74e-47e8-90a5-555d01efa09e

2024-08-01T00:01:00.010Z

11.0

22.1

04ef86bd-ae54-4930-8006-ae6d91f80692

789f706a-f74e-47e8-90a5-555d01efa09e

2024-08-01T00:02:00.010Z

14.0

52.7

File must contain mandatory columns (managedObjectId, measuringNodeId,_time) followed by characteristicsInternalId prefixed with C_

Note: All the column names are case sensitive.

Mandatory Field:

  1. managedObjectId:  Combination of technicalObjectNumber, technicalObjectType, SSID combinedly  forms the managedObjectId.
  2. measuringNodeId: Combination of positionID, and categoryName combinedly represents the measuringNodeId.
  3. _time:  _time represents the business timestamp and must be passed in the UTC standards to maintain consistency across the IoT APIs.

    Formatter: yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ss.SSS'Z'

    Example of _time format:  2024-08-01T00:01:00.010Z

Dynamic Field:

characteristics InternalId:  Characteristics Id of an indicator for which data need to be ingested. characteristicsInternalId should be prefixed with C_.

Example: If characteristicsInternalId is 1101, then the column name will be C_1101.

 

Mapping Id of Equipment and Indicator

Mapping of managedObjectId (technicalObjectNumber, technicalObjectType, SSID)  to measuringNodeId (positionID, and categoryName) and indicator mapping can be found in SAP helper API Doc.

 

Sample file schema
 
{
  "type" : "record",
  "name" : "table",
  "fields" : [ {
    "name" : "managedObjectId",
    "type" : "string"
  }, {
    "name" : "measuringNodeId",
    "type" : "string"
  }, {
    "name" : "_time",
    "type" : {
      "type" : "long",
      "logicalType" : "timestamp-micros"
    }
  }, {
    "name" : "C_1120",
    "type" : "double"
  }, {
    "name" : "C_1121",
    "type" : "int"
  }, {
    "name" : "C_1122",
    "type" : "string"
  }, {
    "name" : "C_1123",
    "type" : "boolean"
  }, {
    "name" : "C_1124",
    "type" : {
      "type" : "long",
      "logicalType" : "timestamp-micros"
    }
  }, {
    "name" : "C_1125",
    "type" : {
      "type" : "int",
      "logicalType" : "date"
    }
  } ]
}

 

Sample snippet to generate parquet files.

Following are snippets that will help generate parquet files. The consumer can refer to them or generate the parquet files using any other mechanism.

Java

 

Maven dependencies

<dependency>
<groupId>org.apache.iceberg</groupId>
<artifactId>iceberg-core</artifactId>
<version>1.6.0</version>
</dependency>

<dependency>
<groupId>org.apache.iceberg</groupId>
<artifactId>iceberg-parquet</artifactId>
<version>1.6.0</version>
</dependency>


<dependency>
<groupId>org.apache.parquet</groupId>
<artifactId>parquet-avro</artifactId>
<version>1.14.1</version>
</dependency>


<dependency>
<groupId>org.apache.hadoop</groupId>
<artifactId>hadoop-client</artifactId>
<version>3.4.0</version>
</dependency>

 

Sample Java Class

import org.apache.iceberg.Files;
import org.apache.iceberg.PartitionSpec;
import org.apache.iceberg.Schema;
import org.apache.iceberg.data.GenericRecord;
import org.apache.iceberg.data.parquet.GenericParquetWriter;
import org.apache.iceberg.io.DataWriter;
import org.apache.iceberg.io.OutputFile;
import org.apache.iceberg.parquet.Parquet;
import org.apache.iceberg.types.Types;
import java.io.IOException;
import java.time.LocalDate;
import java.time.OffsetDateTime;
import java.util.ArrayList;
import java.util.HashMap;
import java.util.List;
import java.util.Map;

public class SampleFile {
public static void main(String[] args) {
//generate schema of the file
List<Types.NestedField> fields = new ArrayList<>();
fields.add(Types.NestedField.required(1, "managedObjectId", Types.StringType.get()));
fields.add(Types.NestedField.required(2, "measuringNodeId", Types.StringType.get()));
fields.add(Types.NestedField.required(3, "_time", Types.TimestampType.withZone()));
fields.add(Types.NestedField.required(4, "C_1120", Types.DoubleType.get())); //#NumericFlexible
fields.add(Types.NestedField.required(5, "C_1121", Types.IntegerType.get())); //#Numeric
fields.add(Types.NestedField.required(6, "C_1122", Types.StringType.get())); //#String
fields.add(Types.NestedField.required(7, "C_1123", Types.BooleanType.get())); //#Boolean
fields.add(Types.NestedField.required(8, "C_1124", Types.TimestampType.withZone())); //#Timestamp
fields.add(Types.NestedField.required(9, "C_1125", Types.DateType.get())); //#Date
Schema schema = new Schema(fields);
//provide output path
OutputFile outputFile = Files.localOutput("Test.parquet");
try (DataWriter<GenericRecord> dataWriter =
Parquet.writeData(outputFile)
.schema(schema)
.withSpec(PartitionSpec.unpartitioned())
.createWriterFunc(GenericParquetWriter::buildWriter)
.overwrite()
.build()) {
GenericRecord genericRecord = GenericRecord.create(schema);
//prepare data
Map<String, Object> map = new HashMap<>();
map.put("managedObjectId", "MO_12345");
map.put("measuringNodeId", "MN_9876");
map.put("_time", OffsetDateTime.parse("2024-07-30T05:15:36.303Z"));
map.put("C_1120", 123.45);
map.put("C_1121", 123);
map.put("C_1122", "Test");
map.put("C_1123", true);
map.put("C_1124",OffsetDateTime.parse("2024-07-20T10:15:36.303Z"));
map.put("C_1125", LocalDate.parse("2024-07-20"));
GenericRecord record = genericRecord.copy(map);
//write data to file
dataWriter.write(record);
} catch (IOException e) {
throw new RuntimeException(e);
}
}
}

 

Python

Creating parquet file

 

Sample Python code to create parquet file

import pandas as pd
import pyarrow as pa
import pyarrow.parquet as pq

# Define the schema
schema = pa.schema([
('managedObjectId', pa.string(),), #String
('measuringNodeId',pa.string()), #String
('_time', pa.timestamp('ms')) , #Timestamp
('C_1101', pa.int32()), #Numeric
('C_1102', pa.float64()) , #NumericFlexible
])

# Sample data
data = {
'managedObjectId': ['04ef86bd-ae54-4930-8006-ae6d91f80692','04ef86bd-ae54-4930-8006-ae6d91f80692','04ef86bd-ae54-4930-8006-ae6d91f80692'],
'measuringNodeId': ['789f706a-f74e-47e8-90a5-555d01efa09e','789f706a-f74e-47e8-90a5-555d01efa09e','789f706a-f74e-47e8-90a5-555d01efa09e'],
'_time': [pd.Timestamp('2023-05-01T00:01:00.010Z'),pd.Timestamp('2023-05-01T00:02:00.010Z'),pd.Timestamp('2023-05-01T00:03:00.002Z')],
'C_1101':[10,11,12],
'C_1102': [121.2,1111.0,1.2]

}

# Create DataFrame
df = pd.DataFrame(data)
# Convert DataFrame to PyArrow Table with specified schema
table = pa.Table.from_pandas(df, schema=schema)
# Save PyArrow Table as Parquet file
pq.write_table(table, 'ts_data_demo.parquet', compression='GZIP')

print("Parquet file created successfully.")

 

Creating parquet file from csv data

 

Sample data CSV
managedObjectId,measuringNodeId,_time,C_1101,C_1102
04ef86bd-ae54-4930-8006-ae6d91f80692,789f706a-f74e-47e8-90a5-555d01efa09e,2023-05-01T00:01:00.01,10,121.2
04ef86bd-ae54-4930-8006-ae6d91f80692,789f706a-f74e-47e8-90a5-555d01efa09e,2023-05-01T00:02:00.01,11,122.2)

 

Sample Python code to convert csv to parquet file

import pandas as pd
import pyarrow as pa
import pyarrow.parquet as pq

# Define your schema
schema = pa.schema([
('managedObjectId', pa.string(),), #String
('measuringNodeId',pa.string()), #String
('_time', pa.timestamp('ms')), #Timestamp
('C_1101, pa.int32()), #Numeric
('C_1102, pa.float64()) #NumericFlexible

])

csv_file_path = '/<base-path>/test-data.csv'
df = pd.read_csv(csv_file_path,index_col=0)
df['_time'] =pd.to_datetime(df['_time'],format='%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S.%f')
print(df)
table = pa.Table.from_pandas(df, schema=schema)

parquet_file_path = '/<base-path>/output_file1.parquet'
pq.write_table(table, parquet_file_path)

entry.title: HOW TO DISABLE ONLY SINGLE CELL OF CHECKBOX AFTER CLICKING ON CHECKDATA BUTTON
entry.content:
I WANT TO DISABLE SINGLE CELL OF CHECKBOXES WHICH IS SELECTED BY USER AND ENTERED DATA IN MAKTX OF SAME ROW DISPLAYED IN REUSE ALV GRID DISPLAY LVC AFTER CLICKING ON A BUTTON NAMED CHECKDATA BY USERCOMMAND. I HAVE USED LVC_S_STYLE FOR THIS AND CL_GUI_ALV_GRID=>MC_STYLE_DISABLED. 

 


CODE FOR REFERENCE: 

*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*& Report Z_TASK4_OOPS
*&
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*&
*&
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*

REPORT z_task4_oops.

TABLES: mara, marc, mard, makt.

TYPE-POOLS: slis.

TYPES : BEGIN OF TY_FINAL,
matnr TYPE mara-matnr,
ersda TYPE mara-ersda,
ernam TYPE mara-ernam,
mtart TYPE mara-mtart,
werks TYPE marc-werks,
lgort TYPE mard-lgort,
xchar TYPE marc-xchar,
maktx TYPE makt-maktx,
check TYPE c,
line_color(4),
col_field TYPE LVC_T_SCOL,
celltab TYPE lvc_t_styl,
END OF TY_FINAL.


TYPES: BEGIN OF ty_mara,
matnr TYPE mara-matnr,
ersda TYPE mara-ersda,
ernam TYPE mara-ernam,
mtart TYPE mara-mtart,
END OF ty_mara.

TYPES : BEGIN OF ty_makt,
matnr TYPE makt-matnr,
maktx TYPE makt-maktx,
END OF ty_makt.

TYPES : BEGIN OF ty_marc,
matnr TYPE marc-matnr,
werks TYPE marc-werks,
xchar TYPE marc-xchar,
END OF ty_marc.

TYPES : BEGIN OF ty_mard,
matnr TYPE mard-matnr,
werks TYPE mard-werks,
lgort TYPE mard-lgort,
END OF ty_mard.

TYPES : BEGIN OF ty_mtart,
mtart TYPE mara-mtart,
END OF ty_mtart.

TYPES : BEGIN OF ty_matnr,
matnr TYPE mara-matnr,
END OF ty_matnr.

*DATA DECLARATIONS

DATA: LT_FINAL TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF TY_FINAL INITIAL SIZE 0,
LS_FINAL LIKE LINE OF LT_FINAL.
DATA :lt_mara TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF ty_mara,
ls_mara TYPE ty_mara.
DATA :lt_makt TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF ty_makt,
ls_makt TYPE ty_makt.
DATA :lt_marc TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF ty_marc,
ls_marc TYPE ty_marc.
DATA :lt_mard TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF ty_mard,
ls_mard TYPE ty_mard.
DATA: lt_event TYPE slis_t_event,
ls_event TYPE slis_alv_event.
DATA: LS_COL_FIELD TYPE SLIS_SPECIALCOL_ALV.
DATA: lt_fcat TYPE lvc_t_fcat,
ls_fcat TYPE lvc_s_fcat.
DATA: lt_layout TYPE LVC_S_LAYO,
LS_TABCOLOR TYPE LVC_S_SCOL,
LS_STYLE TYPE LVC_S_STYL,
LT_STYLE TYPE LVC_T_STYL,
LO_GRID TYPE REF TO CL_GUI_ALV_GRID,
G_CUSTOM_CONTAINER TYPE REF TO CL_GUI_CUSTOM_CONTAINER,
LT_FINAL_TEMP LIKE LT_FINAL,
pos TYPE i,
FIELDCAT TYPE SLIS_T_FIELDCAT_ALV WITH HEADER LINE.

 

SELECTION-SCREEN BEGIN OF BLOCK b1 WITH FRAME.
PARAMETER: p_matnr TYPE mara-matnr.
SELECT-OPTIONS: s_mtart FOR mara-mtart.
SELECTION-SCREEN END OF BLOCK b1.

START-OF-SELECTION.
PERFORM get_material.
PERFORM get_plant.
PERFORM get_storage.
PERFORM get_description.
PERFORM prepare_output.

END-OF-SELECTION.
PERFORM get_layout.
PERFORM get_fieldcat.
PERFORM display_alv.

*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*& Form GET_FIELDCAT
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
* text
*----------------------------------------------------------------------*
* --> p1 text
* <-- p2 text
*----------------------------------------------------------------------*
FORM get_fieldcat .

pos = pos + 1.
CLEAR lt_fcat.
CLEAR ls_fcat.

ls_fcat-fieldname = 'CHECK'.
ls_fcat-coltext = ' '.
ls_fcat-checkbox = 'X'.
ls_fcat-col_pos = pos.
ls_fcat-tabname = 'LT_FINAL'.
ls_fcat-edit = 'X'.
ls_fcat-style = ls_fcat-style bit-xor
cl_gui_alv_grid=>MC_STYLE_disabled.
APPEND ls_fcat TO lt_fcat.
CLEAR ls_fcat.

ls_fcat-fieldname = 'MATNR'.
ls_fcat-coltext = 'MATERIAL NUMBER'.
ls_fcat-tabname = 'LT_FINAL'.
ls_fcat-col_pos = pos.
APPEND ls_fcat TO lt_fcat.
CLEAR ls_fcat.


ls_fcat-fieldname = 'WERKS'.
ls_fcat-coltext = 'Plant'.
ls_fcat-tabname = 'LT_FINAL'.
ls_fcat-col_pos = pos.
APPEND ls_fcat TO lt_fcat.
CLEAR ls_fcat.

ls_fcat-fieldname = 'lGORT'.
ls_fcat-coltext = 'Storage Location'.
ls_fcat-tabname = 'LT_FINAL'.
ls_fcat-col_pos = pos.
APPEND ls_fcat TO lt_fcat.
CLEAR ls_fcat.

ls_fcat-fieldname = 'MTART'.
ls_fcat-coltext = 'Material Type'.
ls_fcat-tabname = 'LT_FINAL'.
ls_fcat-col_pos = pos.
APPEND ls_fcat TO lt_fcat.
CLEAR ls_fcat.

ls_fcat-fieldname = 'XCHAR'.
ls_fcat-coltext = 'Batch No.'.
ls_fcat-col_pos = pos.
ls_fcat-tabname = 'LT_FINAL'.
LS_FCAT-emphasize = 'C500'.
APPEND ls_fcat TO lt_fcat.
CLEAR ls_fcat.

ls_fcat-fieldname = 'MAKTX'.
ls_fcat-coltext = 'Material Description'.
ls_fcat-tabname = 'LT_FINAL'.
ls_fcat-edit = 'X'.
ls_fcat-col_pos = pos.
APPEND ls_fcat TO lt_fcat.
CLEAR ls_fcat.


ENDFORM. " GET_FIELDCAT

 


*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*& Form GET_MATERIAL
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
* text
*----------------------------------------------------------------------*
* --> p1 text
* <-- p2 text
*----------------------------------------------------------------------*
FORM get_material .

SELECT matnr ersda ernam mtart
FROM mara
INTO TABLE lt_mara.
* WHERE MATNR = P_MATNR
* AND MTART IN S_MTART.
IF sy-subrc IS INITIAL.
SORT lt_mara BY matnr.
ENDIF.

ENDFORM. " GET_MATERIAL


*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*& Form GET_PLANT
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
* text
*----------------------------------------------------------------------*
* --> p1 text
* <-- p2 text
*----------------------------------------------------------------------*
FORM get_plant .
IF lt_mara IS NOT INITIAL.
SELECT matnr werks xchar
FROM marc
INTO TABLE lt_marc
FOR ALL ENTRIES IN lt_mara
WHERE matnr = lt_mara-matnr.
IF sy-subrc IS INITIAL.
SORT lt_marc BY matnr.
ENDIF.
ENDIF.
ENDFORM. " GET_PLANT


*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*& Form GET_STORAGE
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
* text
*----------------------------------------------------------------------*
* --> p1 text
* <-- p2 text
*----------------------------------------------------------------------*
FORM get_storage .
IF lt_marc IS NOT INITIAL.
SELECT matnr werks lgort
FROM mard
INTO TABLE lt_mard
FOR ALL ENTRIES IN lt_marc
WHERE matnr = lt_marc-matnr
AND werks = lt_marc-werks.
IF sy-subrc IS INITIAL.
SORT lt_mard BY matnr.
ENDIF.
ENDIF.
ENDFORM. " GET_STORAGE


*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*& Form GET_DESCRIPTION
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
* text
*----------------------------------------------------------------------*
* --> p1 text
* <-- p2 text
*----------------------------------------------------------------------*
FORM get_description .
IF lt_mara IS NOT INITIAL.
SELECT matnr maktx
FROM makt
INTO TABLE lt_makt
FOR ALL ENTRIES IN lt_mara
WHERE matnr = lt_mara-matnr
AND spras = 0.
IF sy-subrc IS INITIAL.
SORT lt_makt BY matnr.
ENDIF.
ENDIF.
ENDFORM. " GET_DESCRIPTION


*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*& Form PREPARE_OUTPUT
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
* text
*----------------------------------------------------------------------*
* --> p1 text
* <-- p2 text
*----------------------------------------------------------------------*
FORM prepare_output .
LOOP AT lt_mara INTO ls_mara.
ls_final-matnr = ls_mara-matnr.
ls_final-ersda = ls_mara-matnr.
ls_final-ernam = ls_mara-ernam.
ls_final-mtart = ls_mara-mtart.

READ TABLE lt_makt INTO ls_makt
WITH KEY matnr = ls_mara-matnr.
IF sy-subrc IS INITIAL.
ls_final-maktx = ls_makt-maktx.
ENDIF.

READ TABLE lt_marc INTO ls_marc
WITH KEY matnr = ls_mara-matnr.
IF sy-subrc = 0.
ls_final-werks = ls_marc-werks.
ls_final-xchar = ls_marc-xchar.
ENDIF.

READ TABLE lt_mard INTO ls_mard
WITH KEY matnr = ls_marc-matnr.
IF sy-subrc = 0.
ls_final-lgort = ls_mard-lgort.
ENDIF.

APPEND ls_final TO lt_final.
CLEAR ls_final.

ENDLOOP.

LOOP AT LT_FINAL INTO LS_FINAL WHERE MTART = 'ROH'.
CLEAR LS_TABCOLOR.
LS_TABCOLOR-fname = 'MTART'.
LS_TABCOLOR-color-col = 6.
APPEND LS_TABCOLOR TO LS_FINAL-col_field.
MODIFY LT_FINAL FROM LS_FINAL.

ENDLOOP.

ENDFORM. " PREPARE_OUTPUT


*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*& Form DISPLAY_ALV
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
* text
*----------------------------------------------------------------------*
* --> p1 text
* <-- p2 text
*----------------------------------------------------------------------*
FORM display_alv .

CALL FUNCTION 'REUSE_ALV_GRID_DISPLAY_LVC'
EXPORTING
i_callback_program = sy-repid
I_CALLBACK_PF_STATUS_SET = 'PF_STATUS'
I_CALLBACK_USER_COMMAND = 'USER_COMMAND'
* I_CALLBACK_TOP_OF_PAGE = ' '
* I_CALLBACK_HTML_TOP_OF_PAGE = ' '
* I_CALLBACK_HTML_END_OF_LIST = ' '
* I_STRUCTURE_NAME =
* I_BACKGROUND_ID = ' '
* I_GRID_TITLE =
* I_GRID_SETTINGS =
is_layout_lvc = lt_layout
it_fieldcat_lvc = lt_fcat
TABLES
t_outtab = lt_final
EXCEPTIONS
PROGRAM_ERROR = 1
OTHERS = 2.

IF sy-subrc <> 0.
* Implement suitable error handling here
ENDIF.


ENDFORM. " DISPLAY_ALV


*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*& Form GET_LAYOUT
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
* text
*----------------------------------------------------------------------*
* --> p1 text
* <-- p2 text
*----------------------------------------------------------------------*
FORM get_layout .

lt_layout-zebra = 'X'.
lt_layout-col_opt = 'X'.
LT_LAYOUT-cwidth_opt = 'X'.
LT_LAYOUT-info_fname = 'LINE_COLOR'.
LT_LAYOUT-ctab_fname = 'COL_FIELD'.
LT_LAYOUT-stylefname = 'CELLTAB'.

ENDFORM. " GET_LAYOUT


FORM pf_status USING rt_extab TYPE slis_t_extab.

SET PF-STATUS 'STANDARD' EXCLUDING rt_extab.

ENDFORM.


FORM user_command USING r_ucomm LIKE sy-ucomm
rs_selfield TYPE slis_selfield.

Data ref1 type ref to cl_gui_alv_grid.
CALL FUNCTION 'GET_GLOBALS_FROM_SLVC_FULLSCR'
IMPORTING
E_GRID = ref1.
call method ref1->check_changed_data.

CASE R_UCOMM.
WHEN 'CHECKDATA'.
LS_STYLE-fieldname = 'CHECK'.
LS_STYLE-STYLE = cl_gui_alv_grid=>mc_style_disabled.

APPEND LS_STYLE TO LS_FINAL-CELLTAB.
MODIFY LT_FINAL FROM LS_FINAL INDEX 5 TRANSPORTING CELLTAB.

 

* READ TABLE LT_FINAL INTO LS_FINAL INDEX RS_SELFIELD-TABINDEX." WITH KEY CHECK = 'X'.
* IF LS_FINAL-CHECK = 'X' AND LS_FINAL-MAKTX NE ' '.

* DATA: ANS TYPE C.
*
* CALL FUNCTION 'POPUP_WITH_2_BUTTONS_TO_CHOOSE'
* EXPORTING
** DEFAULTOPTION = '1'
* diagnosetext1 = ' '
** DIAGNOSETEXT2 = ' '
** DIAGNOSETEXT3 = ' '
* textline1 = 'INPUT IS INVALID'
** TEXTLINE2 = ' '
** TEXTLINE3 = ' '
* text_option1 = 'YES'
* text_option2 = 'NO'
* titel = ' '
* IMPORTING
* ANSWER = ANS
* .
* IF ANS = '1'.
*
* ELSEIF ANS = '2'.
* LEAVE TO SCREEN 0.
* ENDIF.

WHEN 'EXIT' OR 'CANCEL'.
LEAVE PROGRAM.
WHEN 'BACK'.
LEAVE PROGRAM.
WHEN '&IC1'.
READ TABLE lt_final INTO ls_final INDEX rs_selfield-tabindex.
CASE rs_selfield-fieldname.
WHEN 'MATNR'.
IF ls_final-matnr IS NOT INITIAL.
SET PARAMETER ID 'MAT' FIELD ls_final-matnr.
CALL TRANSACTION 'MM03' AND SKIP FIRST SCREEN.
ENDIF.
ENDCASE.
ENDCASE.

ENDFORM.

entry.title: Stock on Hand using 2LIS_03_BF
entry.content:
Hi There,

I have a requirement to calculate the following , stock on hand for a given day using a SQL given we have the following extractors enabled  2LIS_03_BF (MSEG) & 2LIS_03_UM

  • Inventory QTY
  • Inventory unit cost
  • Inventory Value
  • In network in transit Qty
  • In network in transit Value

Any suggestions or  any help would be much appreciated.  The client does not want to use MATDOC and MATDOC_EXTRACT for their reporting but would like to use the above two extractors.

 

Thanks

Nick

entry.title: HOW TO DISABLE OR GREYOUT SINGLE CELL CHECKBOX WHEN CHECKDATA BUTTON IS CLICKED
entry.content:
I WANT TO DISABLE ONLY SINGLE CHECKBOX  WHICH USER SELECTS FROM WHOLE COLUMN WHILE ENTERING DATA INTO MAKTX AND CLICK ON CHECKDATA BUTTON. I AM USING LVC_S_STYLE AND REUSE ALV GRID DISPLAY LVC WITH CL_GUI_ALV_GRID=>MC_STYLE_DISABLED.     THANKS!

 

CODE FOR REFERENCE: 

 

*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*& Report  Z_TASK4_OOPS
*&
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*&
*&
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*

REPORT z_task4_oops.

TABLES: mara, marc, mard, makt.

TYPE-POOLS: slis.

TYPES : BEGIN OF TY_FINAL,
        matnr TYPE mara-matnr,
        ersda TYPE mara-ersda,
        ernam TYPE mara-ernam,
        mtart TYPE mara-mtart,
        werks TYPE marc-werks,
        lgort TYPE mard-lgort,
        xchar TYPE marc-xchar,
        maktx TYPE makt-maktx,
        check TYPE c,
        line_color(4),
        col_field TYPE LVC_T_SCOL,
        celltab TYPE lvc_t_styl,
        END OF TY_FINAL.


TYPES: BEGIN OF ty_mara,
           matnr TYPE mara-matnr,
           ersda TYPE mara-ersda,
           ernam TYPE mara-ernam,
           mtart TYPE mara-mtart,
       END OF ty_mara.

TYPES :  BEGIN OF ty_makt,
         matnr TYPE makt-matnr,
         maktx TYPE makt-maktx,
  END OF ty_makt.

TYPES :  BEGIN OF ty_marc,
         matnr TYPE marc-matnr,
         werks TYPE marc-werks,
         xchar TYPE marc-xchar,
         END OF ty_marc.

TYPES :  BEGIN OF ty_mard,
        matnr TYPE mard-matnr,
        werks TYPE mard-werks,
        lgort TYPE mard-lgort,
END OF ty_mard.

TYPES : BEGIN OF ty_mtart,
  mtart TYPE mara-mtart,
  END OF ty_mtart.

TYPES : BEGIN OF ty_matnr,
  matnr TYPE mara-matnr,
  END OF ty_matnr.

*DATA DECLARATIONS

DATA: LT_FINAL TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF TY_FINAL INITIAL SIZE 0,
      LS_FINAL LIKE LINE OF LT_FINAL.
DATA :lt_mara TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF ty_mara,
      ls_mara TYPE  ty_mara.
DATA :lt_makt TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF ty_makt,
      ls_makt TYPE  ty_makt.
DATA :lt_marc TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF ty_marc,
      ls_marc TYPE  ty_marc.
DATA :lt_mard TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF ty_mard,
      ls_mard TYPE  ty_mard.
DATA: lt_event TYPE slis_t_event,
      ls_event TYPE slis_alv_event.
DATA: LS_COL_FIELD TYPE SLIS_SPECIALCOL_ALV.
DATA: lt_fcat TYPE lvc_t_fcat,
      ls_fcat TYPE lvc_s_fcat.
DATA: lt_layout TYPE LVC_S_LAYO,
      LS_TABCOLOR TYPE LVC_S_SCOL,
      LS_STYLE TYPE LVC_S_STYL,
      LT_STYLE TYPE LVC_T_STYL,
      LO_GRID TYPE REF TO CL_GUI_ALV_GRID,
      G_CUSTOM_CONTAINER TYPE REF TO CL_GUI_CUSTOM_CONTAINER,
      LT_FINAL_TEMP LIKE LT_FINAL,
      pos TYPE i,
      FIELDCAT TYPE SLIS_T_FIELDCAT_ALV WITH HEADER LINE.



SELECTION-SCREEN BEGIN OF BLOCK b1 WITH FRAME.
PARAMETER: p_matnr TYPE mara-matnr.
SELECT-OPTIONS: s_mtart FOR mara-mtart.
SELECTION-SCREEN END OF BLOCK b1.

START-OF-SELECTION.
  PERFORM get_material.
  PERFORM get_plant.
  PERFORM get_storage.
  PERFORM get_description.
  PERFORM prepare_output.

END-OF-SELECTION.
  PERFORM get_layout.
  PERFORM get_fieldcat.
  PERFORM display_alv.

*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*&      Form  GET_FIELDCAT
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*       text
*----------------------------------------------------------------------*
*  -->  p1        text
*  <--  p2        text
*----------------------------------------------------------------------*
FORM get_fieldcat .

  pos = pos + 1.
  CLEAR lt_fcat.
  CLEAR ls_fcat.

  ls_fcat-fieldname = 'CHECK'.
  ls_fcat-coltext = ' '.
  ls_fcat-checkbox = 'X'.
  ls_fcat-col_pos = pos.
  ls_fcat-tabname = 'LT_FINAL'.
  ls_fcat-edit = 'X'.
  ls_fcat-style = ls_fcat-style bit-xor
                  cl_gui_alv_grid=>MC_STYLE_disabled.
  APPEND ls_fcat TO lt_fcat.
  CLEAR ls_fcat.

  ls_fcat-fieldname = 'MATNR'.
  ls_fcat-coltext = 'MATERIAL NUMBER'.
  ls_fcat-tabname = 'LT_FINAL'.
  ls_fcat-col_pos = pos.
  APPEND ls_fcat TO lt_fcat.
  CLEAR ls_fcat.


  ls_fcat-fieldname = 'WERKS'.
  ls_fcat-coltext = 'Plant'.
  ls_fcat-tabname = 'LT_FINAL'.
  ls_fcat-col_pos = pos.
  APPEND ls_fcat TO lt_fcat.
  CLEAR ls_fcat.

  ls_fcat-fieldname = 'lGORT'.
  ls_fcat-coltext = 'Storage Location'.
  ls_fcat-tabname = 'LT_FINAL'.
  ls_fcat-col_pos = pos.
  APPEND ls_fcat TO lt_fcat.
  CLEAR ls_fcat.

  ls_fcat-fieldname = 'MTART'.
  ls_fcat-coltext = 'Material Type'.
  ls_fcat-tabname = 'LT_FINAL'.
  ls_fcat-col_pos = pos.
  APPEND ls_fcat TO lt_fcat.
  CLEAR ls_fcat.

  ls_fcat-fieldname = 'XCHAR'.
  ls_fcat-coltext = 'Batch No.'.
  ls_fcat-col_pos = pos.
  ls_fcat-tabname = 'LT_FINAL'.
  LS_FCAT-emphasize = 'C500'.
  APPEND ls_fcat TO lt_fcat.
  CLEAR ls_fcat.

  ls_fcat-fieldname = 'MAKTX'.
  ls_fcat-coltext = 'Material Description'.
  ls_fcat-tabname = 'LT_FINAL'.
  ls_fcat-edit = 'X'.
  ls_fcat-col_pos = pos.
  APPEND ls_fcat TO lt_fcat.
  CLEAR ls_fcat.


ENDFORM.                    " GET_FIELDCAT




*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*&      Form  GET_MATERIAL
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*       text
*----------------------------------------------------------------------*
*  -->  p1        text
*  <--  p2        text
*----------------------------------------------------------------------*
FORM get_material .

  SELECT matnr ersda ernam mtart
    FROM mara
    INTO TABLE lt_mara.
*     WHERE MATNR = P_MATNR
*     AND MTART IN S_MTART.
  IF sy-subrc IS INITIAL.
    SORT lt_mara BY matnr.
  ENDIF.

ENDFORM.                    " GET_MATERIAL


*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*&      Form  GET_PLANT
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*       text
*----------------------------------------------------------------------*
*  -->  p1        text
*  <--  p2        text
*----------------------------------------------------------------------*
FORM get_plant .
  IF lt_mara IS NOT INITIAL.
    SELECT matnr werks xchar
      FROM marc
      INTO TABLE lt_marc
      FOR ALL ENTRIES IN lt_mara
      WHERE matnr = lt_mara-matnr.
    IF sy-subrc IS INITIAL.
      SORT lt_marc BY matnr.
    ENDIF.
  ENDIF.
ENDFORM.                    " GET_PLANT


*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*&      Form  GET_STORAGE
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*       text
*----------------------------------------------------------------------*
*  -->  p1        text
*  <--  p2        text
*----------------------------------------------------------------------*
FORM get_storage .
  IF lt_marc IS NOT INITIAL.
    SELECT matnr werks lgort
      FROM mard
      INTO TABLE lt_mard
      FOR ALL ENTRIES IN lt_marc
      WHERE matnr = lt_marc-matnr
      AND werks = lt_marc-werks.
    IF sy-subrc IS INITIAL.
      SORT lt_mard BY matnr.
    ENDIF.
  ENDIF.
ENDFORM.                    " GET_STORAGE


*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*&      Form  GET_DESCRIPTION
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*       text
*----------------------------------------------------------------------*
*  -->  p1        text
*  <--  p2        text
*----------------------------------------------------------------------*
FORM get_description .
  IF lt_mara IS NOT INITIAL.
    SELECT matnr maktx
      FROM makt
      INTO TABLE lt_makt
      FOR ALL ENTRIES IN lt_mara
      WHERE matnr = lt_mara-matnr
     AND spras = 0.
    IF sy-subrc IS INITIAL.
      SORT lt_makt BY matnr.
    ENDIF.
  ENDIF.
ENDFORM.                    " GET_DESCRIPTION


*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*&      Form  PREPARE_OUTPUT
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*       text
*----------------------------------------------------------------------*
*  -->  p1        text
*  <--  p2        text
*----------------------------------------------------------------------*
FORM prepare_output .
  LOOP AT lt_mara INTO ls_mara.
    ls_final-matnr = ls_mara-matnr.
    ls_final-ersda = ls_mara-matnr.
    ls_final-ernam = ls_mara-ernam.
    ls_final-mtart = ls_mara-mtart.

    READ TABLE lt_makt INTO ls_makt
    WITH KEY matnr = ls_mara-matnr.
    IF sy-subrc IS INITIAL.
      ls_final-maktx = ls_makt-maktx.
    ENDIF.

    READ TABLE lt_marc INTO ls_marc
    WITH KEY matnr = ls_mara-matnr.
    IF sy-subrc = 0.
      ls_final-werks = ls_marc-werks.
      ls_final-xchar = ls_marc-xchar.
    ENDIF.

    READ TABLE lt_mard INTO ls_mard
    WITH KEY matnr = ls_marc-matnr.
    IF sy-subrc = 0.
      ls_final-lgort = ls_mard-lgort.
    ENDIF.

    APPEND ls_final TO lt_final.
    CLEAR ls_final.

  ENDLOOP.

  LOOP AT LT_FINAL INTO LS_FINAL WHERE MTART = 'ROH'.
       CLEAR LS_TABCOLOR.
       LS_TABCOLOR-fname = 'MTART'.
       LS_TABCOLOR-color-col = 6.
       APPEND LS_TABCOLOR TO LS_FINAL-col_field.
       MODIFY LT_FINAL FROM LS_FINAL.

  ENDLOOP.

ENDFORM.                    " PREPARE_OUTPUT


*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*&      Form  DISPLAY_ALV
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*       text
*----------------------------------------------------------------------*
*  -->  p1        text
*  <--  p2        text
*----------------------------------------------------------------------*
FORM display_alv .

  CALL FUNCTION 'REUSE_ALV_GRID_DISPLAY_LVC'
    EXPORTING
      i_callback_program = sy-repid
     I_CALLBACK_PF_STATUS_SET          = 'PF_STATUS'
     I_CALLBACK_USER_COMMAND           = 'USER_COMMAND'
*     I_CALLBACK_TOP_OF_PAGE            = ' '
*     I_CALLBACK_HTML_TOP_OF_PAGE       = ' '
*     I_CALLBACK_HTML_END_OF_LIST       = ' '
*     I_STRUCTURE_NAME   =
*     I_BACKGROUND_ID    = ' '
*     I_GRID_TITLE       =
*     I_GRID_SETTINGS    =
      is_layout_lvc      = lt_layout
      it_fieldcat_lvc    = lt_fcat
    TABLES
      t_outtab           = lt_final
   EXCEPTIONS
     PROGRAM_ERROR                     = 1
     OTHERS                            = 2.

  IF sy-subrc <> 0.
* Implement suitable error handling here
  ENDIF.


ENDFORM.                    " DISPLAY_ALV


*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*&      Form  GET_LAYOUT
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*       text
*----------------------------------------------------------------------*
*  -->  p1        text
*  <--  p2        text
*----------------------------------------------------------------------*
FORM get_layout .

  lt_layout-zebra = 'X'.
  lt_layout-col_opt = 'X'.
  LT_LAYOUT-cwidth_opt = 'X'.
  LT_LAYOUT-info_fname = 'LINE_COLOR'.
  LT_LAYOUT-ctab_fname = 'COL_FIELD'.
  LT_LAYOUT-stylefname = 'CELLTAB'.

ENDFORM.                    " GET_LAYOUT


FORM pf_status USING rt_extab TYPE slis_t_extab.

  SET PF-STATUS 'STANDARD' EXCLUDING rt_extab.

ENDFORM.


FORM user_command USING r_ucomm LIKE sy-ucomm
      rs_selfield TYPE slis_selfield.

  Data ref1 type ref to cl_gui_alv_grid.
  CALL FUNCTION 'GET_GLOBALS_FROM_SLVC_FULLSCR'
  IMPORTING
  E_GRID = ref1.
  call method ref1->check_changed_data.

CASE R_UCOMM.
    WHEN 'CHECKDATA'.
    LS_STYLE-fieldname = 'CHECK'.
    LS_STYLE-STYLE = cl_gui_alv_grid=>mc_style_disabled.

    APPEND LS_STYLE TO LS_FINAL-CELLTAB.
    MODIFY LT_FINAL FROM LS_FINAL INDEX 5 TRANSPORTING CELLTAB.



*     READ TABLE LT_FINAL INTO LS_FINAL INDEX RS_SELFIELD-TABINDEX." WITH KEY CHECK = 'X'.
*     IF LS_FINAL-CHECK = 'X' AND LS_FINAL-MAKTX NE ' '.

*      DATA: ANS TYPE C.
*
*       CALL FUNCTION 'POPUP_WITH_2_BUTTONS_TO_CHOOSE'
*         EXPORTING
**          DEFAULTOPTION       = '1'
*           diagnosetext1       = ' '
**          DIAGNOSETEXT2       = ' '
**          DIAGNOSETEXT3       = ' '
*           textline1           = 'INPUT IS INVALID'
**          TEXTLINE2           = ' '
**          TEXTLINE3           = ' '
*           text_option1        = 'YES'
*           text_option2        = 'NO'
*           titel               = ' '
*        IMPORTING
*          ANSWER              = ANS
*                 .
*         IF ANS = '1'.
*
*                ELSEIF ANS = '2'.
*                  LEAVE TO SCREEN 0.
*        ENDIF.

    WHEN 'EXIT' OR 'CANCEL'.
      LEAVE PROGRAM.
    WHEN 'BACK'.
      LEAVE PROGRAM.
    WHEN '&IC1'.
      READ TABLE lt_final INTO ls_final INDEX rs_selfield-tabindex.
      CASE rs_selfield-fieldname.
        WHEN 'MATNR'.
          IF ls_final-matnr IS NOT INITIAL.
            SET PARAMETER ID 'MAT' FIELD ls_final-matnr.
            CALL TRANSACTION 'MM03' AND SKIP FIRST SCREEN.
          ENDIF.
      ENDCASE.
 ENDCASE.

ENDFORM.

entry.title: SAP Installation Server and Microsoft Global Secure Access
entry.content:
Hi, 

I have created a SAP Installation Server for Windows SAP GUI using a virtual machine on Azure, following the guides SAPGUI Installation Server Part 1 - Getting Starte... - SAP Community , SAP GUI Installation Server - SAP Community , SAP GUI for Windows | SAP Help Portal . "Accounts: Guest account status" is enabled on the server as well as "Network Access: Let Everyone permissions apply to anonymous users", using group policy.

I have created a test machine using a virtual machine on Azure on the same vnet on the same subnet. Both virtual machines are joined on the same active directory domain.  All network connections are open between the virtual machines. I have created a package on the server and then installed the package on the client. I changed the package version on the server, and restarted the SAP Update service on the client and automatic workstation update service worked as expected. The new version of the package was dispatched on the client, as expected. 

Then, I performed another test using a windows 11 client that is joined only on Entra (former Azure Active Directory), is managed by InTune, but the users on the PC are hybrid identities (i.e. identities reside both on Active Directory and are also synced on Entra). The client is using Global Secure Access client (What is Global Secure Access? - Global Secure Access | Microsoft Learn) to access the SMB share of the installation server via an application proxy. (Application proxy documentation - Microsoft Entra ID | Microsoft Learn). The SMB share is accessible after MFA authentication using Entra's Conditional Access. 

I managed to get read access on the share and run NwSapSetup.exe and then installed the predefined package. (The user on the PC had administrative privileges).

Then I changed the package version on the server and restarted  SAP Automatic Workstation Update service in order to check if the package is updated automatically. Unfortunatelly, it did not work. The system promted for logging in to Global Secure Access in order to grant access to the share, but after successful authentication the package was not updated. After checking the NwSapAutoWorkstationUpdateService.log on the client:

Logfile: C:\Program Files (x86)\SAP\SapSetup\LOGs\NwSapAutoWorkstationUpdateService.log
Started logging: Sat Aug 17 11:07:05 2024 (Θερινή ώρα GTB)
Operating system: Windows 11 Professional (Build 22621 - Version 22H2)
Executing user: SYSTEM (Administrator)
Workstation name: SAPInstallationServerClientMachine
Windows directory: C:\WINDOWS
Temp directory: C:\WINDOWS\TEMP
Working directory: C:\WINDOWS\system32

Commandline: '"C:\Program Files (x86)\SAP\SAPsetup\Setup\Updater\NwSapAutoWorkstationUpdateService.exe"'
Executable: 'C:\Program Files (x86)\SAP\SAPsetup\Setup\Updater\NwSapAutoWorkstationUpdateService.exe'
Version: 9.0.119.0
ProcessId: 19588
ThreadId: 16244
Physical memory: 5020 MB of 16069 MB free
System Uptime: 1 day, 19:51:26 hours

System libraries information
----------------------------

atl.dll: 3.5.2284.0
comctl32.dll: 5.82.22621.3527
vbscript.dll: 5.812.10240.16384
msvcrt.dll: 7.0.22621.2506
oleaut32.dll: 10.0.22621.3672
shell32.dll: 10.0.22621.3880
shlwapi.dll: 10.0.22621.3672
msi.dll: 5.0.22621.3880

SAPSetup libraries information
-------------------------------

NwSapSetupEngine.dll: 9.0.119.0
NwSapSetupATLCommon.dll: 9.0.119.0
NwSapSetupUi.dll: 9.0.119.0
NwSapFeiUt.dll: 9.0.119.0
NwSapFeiLg.dll: 9.0.119.0

********************************************************************************


11:07:05.301 NwSapsAtlC 1 SapSetup ATL Common Library Loaded
11:07:05.326 NwSapsEngn 1 SapSetup Workstation Engine Library Loaded
11:07:05.339 NwSapFeiUt 1 Process NwSapSetup.exe is not running.
11:07:05.398 NwSapsAtlC 1 Reboot Manager: Evaluated the position of SAPSetup's OnReboot Installation Service as:
Folder 'C:\Program Files (x86)\SAP\SapSetup\OnRebootSvc'
Service File Path: 'C:\Program Files (x86)\SAP\SapSetup\OnRebootSvc\NWSAPSetupOnRebootInstSvc.exe'
11:07:05.398 NwSapsAtlC 1 Reading OnReboot Install Service Configuration from 'C:\Program Files (x86)\SAP\SapSetup\OnRebootSvc'
11:07:05.398 NwSapsAtlC 1 Loaded 0 reboot action records, 0 command(s) to execute on-start, 0 file(s) to delete, 0 file(s) to copy, 0 assembly name(s) and 0 command(s) to execute at end
11:07:05.399 NwSapsAtlC 1 DE Deleted
11:07:05.399 NwSapsAtlC 1 StringTable released
11:07:05.410 NwSapFeiUt 1 Process NwSapSetup.exe is not running.
11:07:05.472 NwAWUServc 1 Will check for updates now...
11:07:05.472 NwSapFeiUt 1 Loading %ProgramFiles%\SAP\SapSetup\Setup\Updater\WkstaUpdater.cfg...
11:07:05.479 NwSapFeiUt 1 Nbr of Servers in section AdminConfiguredServers: 0
11:07:05.480 NwSapFeiUt 1 Nbr of Servers in section SAPSetupIndexedServers: 1
11:07:05.480 NwSapFeiUt 1 Read Server1=\\myserver.mydomain.com\sap\Setup from section SAPSetupIndexedServers
11:07:05.480 NwSapFeiUt 1 Loaded 1 server paths from the source
11:07:05.494 NwSapFeiUt 1 Process NwSapSetup.exe is not running.
11:07:58.182 NwSapsAtlC 1W Server Path \\myserver.mydomain.com\sap\Setup could not be found: Error message: Access is denied. Error code 0x00000005 (5).
11:07:58.182 NwAWUServc 1W Error: \\myserver.mydomain.com\sap\Setup is not a valid SapSetup folder.
11:07:58.182 NwAWUServc 1 No more updates are available.
11:07:58.182 NwAWUServc 1 Service will retry in 24 hours

It seems that service of automatic workstation update service (AWUS), is using a user that cannot be authenticated on the installation server. Switching to the server, I then checked SMB Security event log (Application & Services - Microsoft - Windows - SMBServer - Security), and I am getting 551 (SMB Session Authentication Failure) and 1009 (The server denied anonymous access to the client) event IDs. It seems that the application proxies are trying to access the SMB share using anonymous logon.
User Name: NT AUTHORITY\ANONYMOUS LOGON
Session ID: 0x2C000000007D

I then added NT AUTHORITY\ANONUMOUS LOGON on the installation server's share with full permissions in order to test and the problem still remains.

Any ideas how to make Global Secure Access work with SAP Installation Server for Entra Joined Machines ?

entry.title: Re: Create Alternate GL Hierarchies in SAP Analytics Cloud
entry.content:
@DBalinski 

You will need alternate hierarchies only. There is no way you can rollup the same base member to 2 different nodes within a single hierarchy.

On a side note, If I remember correctly this behavior is same in BPC standard so not sure how was this easy in BPC. In BPC 1 leaf member could rollup to a single node in PARENTH1 column.

Nikhil

entry.title: Re: Business case of cProjects
entry.content:
cProjects (part of the PPM solution) were specifically designed around IT and R&D (NPD-new product development).  They are structured with Phases and Tasks (and roles, milestones, checklists, etc.). cProjects plan, schedule, and manage projects but you can't post actual costs to them.  PPM integrates with core ERP PS by creating a parallel WBS only project so actual costs can be collected.  Actuals are passed to PPM for reporting so it's invisible to the user.

One difference with PS is that cProjects can't create material (components) demands or PRs like PS can.

Look here for more:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/db719753e69f4e8eb9902aaea0fd8471/49d254c3590b3f82e10000000a421138.html

Dear Vedaj_r,

On the weekend (August 17th/18th) there are two different LM events:

- all systems which did not belong to the pre-upgrade will be upgraded from 2405 to 2408 HFC3

- all systems which did belong to the pre-upgrade are already running on 2408 HFC2. These systems will get an update from 2408 HFC2 to 2408 HFC3

At  the end of the weekend all systems are on 2408 HFC3, i.e. all systems are on the same status. 

I hope I have explained it for you. In case of further questions please contact me again. 

Regards, Andreas

entry.title: Re: How to put the BPM Governance Building Blocks to good use
entry.content:
Often the sales process reduces the adoption process to "we'll figure it out" as the cost of software squeezes out the cost of adoption.

This is a sure way to not realize the value of the important asset you just purchased.  

If you're buying SAP Signavio it becomes crucial to adopt using the tools as part of your overall corporate Business Process Management.   

If you don't have a mature well managed and motivated BPM organization, then you need to set up some form of Business Process Management for governance of standards and quality.  Especially with SAP Signavio, as it's true value is full lifecycle management.  With each step depending on the quality of the step before.

With that in mind, Taryn's series on establishing BPM for your enterprise is invaluable.  

I've used it in the field under a range of customer maturity and it has always yielded happier customers and a disproportionate ROI.

entry.title: Can we achieve delivery related billing in Customer advance invoice process using Billing plan
entry.content:
Hi All

We implemented Customer invoice invoice process using Billing plan. It is working fine when full delivery takes place where as when partial delivery takes place the billing qty is copying from Sales Order where they need the billed qty equal lent to delivered qty. 

Process steps:

1. VA01- with billing plan

2. VF01- FAZ - down payment request

3. F-29- Post the down payment

4. Vl01N- Delivery

5. SD final invoice (F2)

There is no issue when single delivery and single invoice because when they are creating invoice system copies the order qty i.e. 10.

where as when partial delivery takes place they create partial invoice in this case also system is copying the full order qty i.e. 10 which is incorrect as the partial delivery qty is 4 out of order qty 10.

next time when remaining delivery qty i.e. 6 is delivered user creates invoice for the 3rd billing plan and system is copying the full order qty i.e. 10. However in both the cases the net value is perfectly fine as it is copying the respective billing plan amount into Billing document.

you can see the partial invoice for 4 delivery qty but in invoice qty = 10

and final delivery is for 6 qty but invoice qty copyiing it from SO 10 qty  However net value is ok.

 

So Business requirement is system should allow to create invoice with ref to delivery so correct delivery qty is copied or at least system should allow to change the invoice qty manually while creating so they can modify the invoice qty according to delivery qty.

Other Information:

Since the billing plan is maintained system does not allow the billing relavance other than I

I searched in Google, found some helpful links but that did not help me to resolve my requirement.

Thanks

entry.title: Re: Two different DM Custom View Plugins are mistakenly c...
entry.content:
Hi MaxSte,

There are few incorrect statements in your question so let me see if I can clear them up for you. 😎 Firstly yes you are allowed to have multiple instances of the same plugin in a POD at the same time however you have to enable a configuration in your custom plugin to allow that to happen. If you go to your components.json file in the designer folder you will see there is a setting called "allowMultipleInstances" which is false by default, set this to true and you can have as many instances of the same plugin as you want.

Secondly the name of the plugin that is displayed in the POD designer doesnt come from the application namespace or from the name that you give it in the service registry, it uses the "title" inside the i18n/builder.properties file and from your screenshots and description I would guess that its the same in both your applications so the POD framework thinks its the same app.

Hope this info helps you.

Thanks

Kevin

PS if you are using my yoman generator tool to create your blank plugin in the first place you will find that one of the questions it asks is do you want to support multiple instances 😉

entry.title: Re: SAP DM User concept on shop floor
entry.content:
There is as always no straight answer to the question other than it depends on your exact requirements particularly in regards to traceability. For example if you want traceability of who did what then the operators will have to log in using unique accounts otherwise you cannot have traceability. If this level of traceability is not required then you could consider using a generic account but this will mean you will lose certain functionality such as time tracking , labor scheduling etc.

Yes I agree it is often very difficult for operators to keep logging into a system especially if they move from one work station to another. When I used to work for a living (before becoming a consultant lol) in a real factory I overcame that issue using "zero clients" (https://www.techtarget.com/searchvirtualdesktop/definition/zero-client) rather than PCs on the shop floor, this meant that the operators desktop would move from station to station with them. This worked really well and several other of our factories copied the approach.

Hope this helps

Thanks

Kevin

entry.title: RISE with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition: Understanding Roles And Responsibility
entry.content:
As an SAP Enterprise Architect, one of the most common discussions we have with customers is about the scope of SAP and that of the Customer when adapting RISE with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition. To help facilitate these discussions, SAP provides several useful resources, including the Onboarding Resource Centre, the Roles and Responsibilities document, and the Roadmap Viewer.

1. Onboarding Resource Centre - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition

2. Roles and Responsibilities - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition

3. Roadmap Viewer - RISE with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition

When moving to RISE with SAP, it's important to understand the roles and responsibilities of both SAP and the customer. SAP's basic 'Standard Service' is provided by SAP's Enterprise Cloud Services (ECS). However, the customer or their System Integrator can deliver all other services marked as 'Optional', 'Additional', and 'Cloud Application Service' in the Roles & Responsibilities documentation. If the customer chooses, SAP can also deliver these services, but in most cases, the SI Partner or Customer IT teams prefer to handle these responsibilities.

 

It's important to note that not all tasks or services listed in the Roles & Responsibilities document are relevant to all customer environments. Infrastructure-related services may vary depending on the deployed infrastructure platform (e.g. Hyperscaler). Certain tasks or services may not be available from SAP or certain SAP third-party providers and may not be available in certain regions. The availability of a specific service may also depend on the characteristics of the specific customer situation (e.g. system size, solution scope, etc.) and must be individually checked and confirmed with an SAP Cloud Architect Advisor (CAA), an SAP Client Delivery Manager (CDM), or with the Private Cloud customer centre team and the Partner Cloud Architect (PCA) from the System Integrator.

For more detailed information and task planning, SAP provides a PDF and Excel version of the Roles & Responsibilities document, available at https://www.sap.com/about/agreements/policies/hec-services.html. The Excel version is particularly useful for scope understanding, task analysis, task planning, and overall program task management purposes.

entry.title: What is the best way to generate FINITE schedule using PPDS heuristics for 3 level BOM and fix peg
entry.content:
I have 3 level BOM (FG-->Semi level 1 - Semi level 2) 

I am running PPDS heuristics in following order in S4 (eppds) 1809

1. SAP_PP_LLC (stage numbering)

2. Product planning (Comp acc Llevl Code)  - ie.MRP run

3. and then SAP_PP_019 (fix pegging)

Then 

4. Run Sequence bottom level using setup group using copy of sequencing heuristics

5. Run Bottom up (SAP_PP_009) at Semi level 2

6. Run Stable forward heuristics (SAP_DS_01) for generating finite schedule 

(Tried to run step 6 before 5 also)   

But NOT getting finite schedule and some orders are thrown out of PPDS horizon (one year out) although there is a lot of capacity before that ...some resources are still overloaded  (btw all resources are defined fully restricted finite)

Fixed pegging is active in global customization and turned for products and setting in heuristics to consider it

Any ideas?

I am ok trying a different heuristics as long as I get finite schedule

Please suggest some options

 

Thanks

NK
